channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He's not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris returns to battleground Pennsylvania with Republicans as Trump pursues Latinos’ votes
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris is campaigning in pivotal Pennsylvania for the second time this week, while Republican nominee Donald Trump continues his effort to win over Hispanic voters during an event on the nation’s largest Spanish-language television network.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia judge blocks rule requiring clerks to hand count number of ballots
item.description: A Georgia judge ruled that a rule to hand count ballots on election night was "too much, too late" to implement for this year's election, just weeks away.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia judge blocks ballot counting rule and says county officials must certify election results
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge has blocked a new rule that requires Georgia Election Day ballots to be counted by hand after the close of voting. The ruling came a day after the same judge ruled that county election officials must certify election results by the deadline set in law.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until new funds are approved
item.description: The SBA’s disaster loan program has run out of money, it announced on Tuesday. The agency expects to receive new funding from Congress, and will continue to accept applications in the meantime.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: People with felony records can now vote in Nebraska — and it could help tip the balance in November
item.description: Nebraska’s high court says people with felony records can register to vote in a decision with implications for the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Margery A. Beck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia judge blocks ballot hand-count rule from taking effect before election
item.description: The rule was set to go into effect just two weeks before the election, and after early voting in the state is underway.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Houston ISD superintendent didn’t illegally funnel state money to out-of-state schools, TEA says
item.description: The Texas Education Agency found that checks mailed to Miles’ charter school network in Colorado were eventually deposited into the network’s Texas account.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris outreach to Black men: New policy, interviews, meetings
item.description: On the economic front, Harris intends to provide one million loans that would be fully forgivable of up to $20,000 to Black entrepreneurs.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Hennepin County achieves 'functional zero' status in ending veteran homelessness
item.description: The designation from several federal agencies indicates a community has brought down the number of veterans experiencing homelessness to a very low level and continues to sustain the effort.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Harris peppered with questions by Charlamagne tha God’s audience
item.description: Vice President Harris was peppered with questions Tuesday about her plan for Black Americans from radio host Charlamagne tha God and his listeners in an interview that’s part of a late-campaign media blitz before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Intel chair frets about foreign actors spreading hurricane, election misinformation
item.description: Senate Intelligence Committee Chair Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Tuesday indicated that he is concerned foreign actors are spreading misinformation related to the recovery from a pair of recent hurricanes and will be ramping up efforts amid the final election sprint.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says it would be a 'smart thing' if he spoke to Putin, though he won't confirm he has
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to say whether he's spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office, as reported in journalist Bob Woodward's latest book. But if the two did speak, Trump said, it would be “a smart thing” for the United States.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Harris to host Detroit town hall in bid to reach Black voters
item.description: The vice president will visit with Black entrepreneurs before the town hall with Charlamagne tha God.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Walz to unveil Harris' plan for rural voters as campaign looks to cut into Trump's edge
item.description: Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz will unveil his ticket’s plans to improve the lives of rural voters as Vice President Kamala Harris looks to cut into former President Donald Trump’s support.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris slams Trump for suggesting military handle 'enemy from within' as they vie for Pennsylvania
item.description: Harris, at a rally in Pennsylvania, called Trump a serious threat to American democracy who is “out for unchecked power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, WILL WEISSERT and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Giant pandas will arrive at D.C.'s National Zoo this week
item.description: After an 11-month wait, giant pandas will populate the zoo once again. A pair of pandas, which left China on FedEx's "Panda Express" cargo jet, are expected to arrive in the D.C. area on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Voters with disabilities are feeling ignored by presidential candidates
item.description: A new report from Rutgers University estimates that about 40.2 million eligible voters in the quickly approaching U.S. presidential election are disabled.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In outreach to Black men, Harris to vow to legalize weed, protect crypto
item.description: Polls show that some Black men may be gravitating toward former President Trump or not vote at all. Vice President Harris and other prominent Democrats are trying to counter that.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: This year's 'October surprise' may be no surprise at all
item.description: It is not uncommon for political commentators to call a late-breaking story during a presidential election an "October surprise" — even when there is little evidence that it mattered all that much.
item.dc:creator: Ron Elving, MPR News

item.title: Hunting for male voters: Walz steps into the field for the pheasant opener
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz had more pheasant hunting companions than usual for this year’s opener near Sleepy Eye, on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hovland and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Who could replace interim NYPD Commissioner Donlon if he resigns?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to resign following a string of high-profile exits from City Hall over the last month, sources told PIX11 News on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
